primary_sources.title: NIST AI 600-1: Artificial Intelligence Risk Management Framework: Generative Artificial Intelligence Profile
primary_sources.role: Supports continuous measurement, monitoring, documentation, and risk-management actions rather than one-time acceptance.

primary_sources.title: JSON Schema Validation: A Vocabulary for Structural Validation of JSON
primary_sources.role: Supports machine-checkable structural constraints while leaving domain truth to independent evidence and tests.

primary_sources.title: W3C PROV Overview
primary_sources.role: Distinguishes provenance records from independent assessments of quality, reliability, or trustworthiness.

finding: Research evidence should fail closed when a claim is structurally incomplete, superseded, future-dated, past its declared revalidation deadline, or lacks an executable falsification route. Structural validity and provenance do not establish claim truth.
design_implication: Every reusable research claim can be passed through a deterministic lifecycle gate before it is admitted into deployment evidence, theory summaries, medicine-facing organization, or cross-team handoffs.

verification.environment: isolated Node runtime using byte-equivalent committed source

falsification_route.test: Integrate the gate into one evidence-consuming workflow and inject expired, superseded, and incomplete claim packets.
falsification_route.failure_signal: Any rejected packet reaches a downstream accepted-evidence state, or a current complete packet is rejected without a documented policy reason.
falsification_route.action: Treat the gate as insufficient, preserve the failing fixture, and revise the acceptance contract before reuse.

limits: The gate validates lifecycle metadata and policy constraints, not scientific or medical truth.
A declared revalidation date is a policy input and may itself be poorly chosen.
A written falsification route is not proof that the test was executed.
No diagnosis or treatment claim is produced or accepted by this artifact.
privacy_boundary: Contains public standards, synthetic fixtures, repository paths, and commit identifiers only; excludes private chats, health data, credentials, and personal identifiers.
